title: Gradient descent and the learning rate
description: Training follows the loss gradient scaled by the learning rate; too high diverges to NaN, too low stalls, and the schedule shapes convergence.
body: Use when tuning optimization or diagnosing convergence. The learning rate is usually the highest-leverage hyperparameter: too high causes loss to oscillate or explode to NaN, too low causes painfully slow or stuck training. Adaptive optimizers (Adam) adjust per-parameter rates but still need a sane base rate and often a warmup/decay schedule. Do not assume a default rate transfers across datasets or batch sizes. Actions: sweep the rate logarithmically and watch the loss curve shape. Validation signals include a smooth descending loss and stable gradients. Risk boundary: a diverging run wastes the whole job; clip gradients and reduce the rate at the first sign of NaN.
